signInInformation.title: Changes to Login Process

description.text:
We have merged the login process for EuroLeagueTV and EuroLeague ID – now a single account lets you access both, plus our Fantasy Challenge game.

**If you previously just had a EuroLeagueTV account, please use the forgot password function to activate your new Euroleague ID account.**

If you previously just had a Euroleague ID account, congratulations! You now also have access to a free tier of videos on EuroLeagueTV.\*

**If you are accessing our Fantasy Challenge via our partner's website, please be informed that our partners do not process, collect nor store any user data of participating users as stated in our Privacy Policy**

signInInformation.asteriskText: *EuroLeagueTV is available worldwide excluding Spain, France, Italy and Monaco.

accountDeletionInfo.text:
Before continuing to delete your EuroLeague ID account, please bear in mind the following:

If you play the Euroleague Fantasy Challenge, you may want to delete your team there first, as it will be more difficult to do that if your account no longer exists. You can do that at [euroleaguefantasy.euroleague.net/en/account](http://euroleaguefantasy.euroleague.net/en/account).

The legal basis for the processing is your consent. Only those third parties to whom Euroleague Properties SA is legally obliged and the companies entrusted with internal management services will have access to the data. You can exercise, at any time, under the terms established by law, your rights of access, rectification or deletion of data, as well as request restrictions on the processing of your personal data, oppose its processing or request the portability of your data, by sending a postal communication to the address indicated above or by writing an e-mail to data.protection.officer@euroleague.net. club.info: Crvena Zvezda, known worldwide by the translated name ‘Red Star’, was founded in 1945 and immediately started its domination with 10 straight domestic championships. Zvezda won a half-dozen trophies in the late 1960s and early 1970s, including a national championship and the 1974 Saporta Cup – its lone European trophy to date. However, Zvezda had to wait until 1993 to win another piece of silverware, the first of the three Serbia and Montenegro championships, before returning to prominence at the continental level in 2006 and 2007 with ULEB Cup quarterfinals appearances. Zvezda won its first of three consecutive Serbian Cups in 2013, advanced to the 2014 EuroCup Semifinals and a year later made its EuroLeague debut. That turned out to be a sign of things to come. Under the guidance of Coach Dejan Radonjic, Zvezda won its first triple-crown, lifting the ABA League, Serbian League and Serbian Cup trophies in 2015. The following season, Zvezda took another step forward by advancing to the 2016 EuroLeague Playoffs, where it put up a great fight against eventual champion CSKA Moscow. While continuing to grab domestic silverware at a rapid rate, including triple crowns in 2017, 2021, 2022 and 2024, Zvezda continued to be competitive in the EuroLeague. Last season, Zvezda’s EuroLeague journey finished in the play-in showdown. Now this ambitious club from the Serbian capital is determined to keep pushing and reach heights it has never reached before.

heroMedia.title: Codi Miller-McIntyre flips the switch as Zvezda ends Fenerbahce’s winning run
heroMedia.summary: The 31-year-old guard brought the energy in the third quarter as Zvezda’s defense suffocated the reigning champion

linkedIds.text: Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade v Fenerbahce Beko Istanbul (2026-03-13T19:00:00.000Z)

content.text:
Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade’s home loss to FC Bayern Munich left behind a wounded beast in search of a bounce-back display that would put the team back on track. And what a better way to kick into gear than by ending the reigning champion’s nine-game winning streak, at the very same arena where they ran into trouble last week.

Zvezda relied on its physicality and suffocating defense in the second half to beat Fenerbahce Beko Istanbul 79-73 at Belgrade Arena on Friday night.

“We went through big pressure,” Sasa Obradovic admitted in his post-game flash interview, recalling the previous week's setback. “The Bayern game put us in a so-so situation, but with a great character, with a great attitude, approach, we did a great job.”

Leading the charge was Codi Miller-McIntyre, who pulled off an all-around performance, bringing the energy when Zvezda needed an injection of intensity in their bid to turn the game around.

The hosts started strong, taking a 10-2 lead, but Fenerbahce soon bounced back to establish a narrow lead before taking a 33-42 advantage into halftime.

At this point, Zvezda looked dull on both ends of the floor, failing to find rhythm on offense and struggling to contain Wade Baldwin and Bonzie Colson, who combined for 22 points before the break.

The guests had the Serbian side exactly where they wanted them, but Miller-McIntyre decided it was time to change gears. Zvezda hit the ground running in the second half, trimming the deficit to 43-46 after a bucket from Jordan Nwora in transition.

With the fans on their feet after Nwora’s layup, the decibels skyrocketed in the arena. Fenerbahce tried to respond on the other end, but Miller-McIntyre put pressure on the ball, got the steal and detonated a two-handed slam in transition.

Saras Jasikevicius, who had warned before the game that his team should not “let the crowd in the game”, called a timeout in his bid to end Zvezda’s momentum. Fener replied with a triple from Colson, but Miller-McIntyre was locked in on both ends of the floor.

Following a miss from Nando De Colo, he picked up the rebound, went coast-to-coast, skied for another ferocious slam and drew a foul from Talen Horton Tucker, trimming the deficit to 48-49.

Zvezda had not even taken the lead and the crowd was buzzing. The momentum had shifted.

View this post on Instagram

A post shared by KK Crvena zvezda Meridianbet (@crvenazvezdakk)

“Like I always say, if we come out with defensive energy, we can beat everybody or have a chance to win any game and that’s what we did in the third quarter,” Miller-McIntyre told EuroLeague TV. “We played a great defensive game.”

Zvezda held Fenerbahce to just 14 points in the third quarter, and 18 in the fourth. The visitors pushed back and went on a 0-9 run midway through the closing period, but Zvezda answered with a 12-0 surge, fueled by Jared Butler and Nwora, to finish the game off.

“A typical playoff game I would say,” Obradovic added. “We raised our aggressiveness in the second half, in defense especially, and that’s why we won the game. On the other hand, a tough opponent. Every possession matters [and] it really did, but we found a way like many other times. Having great energy, maybe we can beat anybody.”

Miller-McIntyre was 2 rebounds shy of completing a triple-double, as he netted 10 points, recorded 12 assists and downed 8 boards in a complete outing.

Having rediscovered its confidence by forcing Fener to its first loss in 20 games across all competitions, Zvezda will now look to consolidate its position in the playoff spots. The competition is fierce in an exciting battle for homecourt advantage in the playoffs, but the Serbian side proved on Friday that it has what it takes to beat literally any EuroLeague side – even the high-flying reigning champion.

linkedId.text: Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade v Fenerbahce Beko Istanbul (2026-03-13T19:00:00.000Z)

articleMetadata.title: Codi Miller-McIntyre flips the switch as Zvezda ends Fenerbahce’s winning run
articleMetadata.description:
Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade’s home loss to FC Bayern Munich left behind a wounded beast in search of a bounce-back display that would put the team back on track. And what a better way to kick into gear than by ending the reigning champion’s nine-game winning streak, at the very same arena where they ran into trouble last week.

Zvezda relied on its physicality and suffocating defense in the second half to beat Fenerbahce Beko Istanbul 79-73 at Belgrade Arena on Friday night.

heroMedia.title: Stats review: Turning up the pressure
heroMedia.summary: Sportradar's Matt Kamalsky dives into the defensive numbers

content.text:
With eight games remaining in the EuroLeague regular season, the early separation found by the teams in the playoff picture has nearly disappeared as the team in ninth place now sits only one game clear of the team in twelfth. While the competitiveness of the league has evolved over time, so too have some of the strategies teams are using to find an edge.

One of the less glaring examples of that is the application of full-court pressure. During the 2015-16 season, the average team sent defenders to guard the ball in the backcourt only 5.1% of the time with many teams only pressing to foul and only three teams pressing on more than 10% of the possessions they faced. This season, 10% is the EuroLeague average with eight teams eclipsing that mark and two teams pressing on over 22% of the possessions they face.

Some of that can be traced to intent. Many of the teams pressing the most a decade ago were hunting deflections to generate turnovers. There was a reluctance for many years to apply pressure beyond half court as a result – it felt risky to give the caliber of guards at this level a running start if things went wrong. However, coaches have found a comfort level using pressure more passively to disrupt tempo, try to get the ball out of certain players' hands in early offense, or simply leverage their depth instead of aggressively trying to force mistakes.

The table above lists the teams picking up full court most frequently this season. For reference, the league average for turnover percentage sits at just over 14% for all possessions. This group represents teams from across the standings, all finding varying degrees of success and implementing their pressure in different ways.

[Paris Basketball](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/paris-basketball/prs/) and [Valencia Basket](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/valencia-basket/pam/) are the two teams picking up the ball over the midcourt line most frequently this season. While Paris has posted similar defensive efficiency numbers whether pressing or not, Valencia has been harder to score on when pressing, often burning time off the shot clock and only having their press broken for transition opportunities 5% of the time – a tiny, impressive number at any level of basketball. Valencia does a stellar job eliminating those outcomes, largely mitigating the risk that made coaches reluctant to apply pressure a decade ago.

While EuroLeague teams typically don't rely on the wide variety of press schemes often seen at lower levels, an occasional trapping zone still pops up here and there. Even without as many drastic variations, coaches create variety in other ways. Some teams deploy specific defenders to hound the ball without committing a second defender, others focus on disrupting the inbound pass, and some, like [Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/crvena-zvezda-meridianbet-belgrade/red/), adjust their pressing frequency from game to game based on their scouting report.

[Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/panathinaikos-aktor-athens/pan/) is perhaps the best example of a team trapping to force mistakes this season, as they have come up with quite a few deflections springing timely traps off the inbound pass or just over half court. Generating a turnover on around a quarter of the possessions they press is an impressive result that makes a couple of extra run-outs here or there a minor footnote.

As the season begins to wind down, the little things teams are doing continue to add up and figure to play a role in who gets into the field and who keeps home-court advantage.

articleMetadata.title: Stats review: Turning up the pressure
articleMetadata.description: With eight games remaining in the EuroLeague regular season, the early separation found by the teams in the playoff picture has nearly disappeared as the team in ninth place now sits only one game clear of the team in twelfth. While the competitiveness of the league has evolved over time, so too have some of the strategies teams are using to find an edge.

heroMedia.title: Bayern stuns Crvena Zvezda on the road with huge defensive effort
heroMedia.summary: The German side held the home team to just 6-of-30 from deep as striking points endured a torrid shooting night

linkedIds.text: Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade v FC Bayern Munich (2026-03-06T19:00:00.000Z)

content.text:
[FC Bayern Munich](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/fc-bayern-munich/mun/) ground out a well-deserved 80-85 win at [Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/crvena-zvezda-meridianbet-belgrade/red/) to keep alive its slim hopes of reaching the postseason while denting the home side’s playoff hopes after forcing the final twist in a titanic tussle on Friday.

Bayern bounced back from failure in the German Cup and a crushing defeat at Real Madrid in the previous round to subdue Zvezda, improving to 13-17 while the Serbian team dropped to 17-13 after shooting a dreadful 6-of-30 from downtown.

Bayern firmly held the upper hand in the opening half and led by as many as 16 points (20-36) as the visiting team’s airtight defense gave Zvezda preciously little room to operate. Zvezda made its opening three from its 10th attempt as sharpshooters [Jordan Nwora](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/jordan-nwora/013363/) and [Codi Miller-McIntyre](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/codi-miller-mcintyre/009048/) struggled all night, making a combined 1-of-11 from long range while [Neno Dimitrijevic](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/neno-dimitrijevic/006085/) could not put a foot wrong at the other end of the court.

content.title: Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade-FC Bayern Munich | Round 30 Highlights | 2025-26 EuroLeague

content.text:
Still, Zvezda came back to life after a 7-0 run, coupled with an audacious triple from [Jared Butler](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/jared-butler/014163/) on the halftime buzzer, slashing the deficit to 43-50 at the interval to pump up the volume in the stands. The hosts then nosed ahead 68-67 late in the third quarter and having beefed up its defense in the fourth, Zvezda carved out a 78-75 advantage heading into the home stretch.

The Serbian side went ice-cold from the floor, however, when the chips were down and Bayern forced the final twist on the back of a 2-10 run with [Xavier Rathan-Mayes](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/xavier-rathan-mayes/013378/) and [Andi Obst](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/andreas-obst/004508/) keeping their nerve to silence a noisy home crowd in the Belgrade Arena.      

“It was very intense from the off and the crowd here always makes it difficult to get into it,” Obst said in his postgame comments. “We managed to keep our cool after Red Star made some runs and in the end we deserved to win. We kept fighting for every ball and the bench did a pretty good job. That’s what this team is about as anybody can make an impact,” added the German international guard, who netted 16 points despite shooting a poor 2-of-8 from deep.

However, Obst found other ways to score from inside and played hard defense which frustrated Zvezda while Dimitrijevic added 11 points, 4 rebounds and as many assists. Rathan-Mayes garnished a sound performance with 13 points and [David McCormack](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/david-mccormack/013386/) chipped in with 10 points and 4 boards for Bayern, which shot 11-of-28 from deep.

“We were coming from a difficult moment after losing in the German Cup and we also played well below par against Real Madrid,” Dimitrijevic said. “We stuck together as a team and that’s our biggest strength. We got some key stops and we’re going to fight until the end to get into those play-in spots.”

X stepped up tonight. 😮‍💨 #CZVBAY pic.twitter.com/Gp35HS9tuk

— FC Bayern Basketball (@FCBBasketball) March 6, 2026

Zvezda head coach Sasa Obradovic was bitterly disappointed with his team’s below-par performance, with the outcome potentially inflicting heavy damage to Zvezda’s bid of getting into the business end of Europe’s premier club competition. 

“This could turn out to be a costly defeat but it’s not easy to play against a good team devoid of pressure,” Obardovic told the postgame press conference. “We didn’t handle the pressure tonight and having struggled offensively, we failed to close shop on defense to make sure way we found a way to win. We just weren’t aggressive enough against a team who knew what they were doing and it cost us in the end.”

[Chima Moneke](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/chima-moneke/012201/) poured in 22 points for a disjointed Zvezda side while Butler and Nwora added 10 apiece, but the hosts ultimately paid the price for missing too many open shots when the chips were down. Bayern head coach Svetsislav Pesic said it was a morale-boosting moment for his outfit, which snapped a two-game losing streak. 

“It will do the team a world of good and even more so than by the result itself, we’re buoyed by the maturity and character we showed tonight,” the veteran Serbian tactician told the Balkan country’s Arena Sport television. “It was a close game and a tough challenge to beat such a strong team as Zvezda on the road, so credit to the lads for pulling off this one.”

heroMedia.title: Phase 2 general public tickets for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad sell out

content.text:
The extraordinary demand for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens Presented by Etihad shows no signs of slowing down, as all tickets made available during Phase 2 of public sales have officially sold out.

When Phase 2 opened on February 26 at 10:00 CET, fans once again responded in massive numbers. Following Phase 1, which saw more than one million fans connect to the ticketing platform and resulted in a complete sellout, the second allocation was also quickly exhausted, reaffirming the immense appeal of European basketball’s premier event.

The overwhelming global interest highlights the unique atmosphere and significance of the Final Four, with fans from across Europe and beyond eager to secure their place in Athens for the season’s ultimate showdown.

Fans who were unable to purchase tickets during General public tickets sale Phase 2 will still have an additional opportunity on February 26 as VIP tickets will be available starting at 14:00 CET.

Fans who were unable to secure tickets during Phase 1 will still have multiple opportunities to attend European basketball’s premier event. The second public ticket sales phase will open on February 26 at 10:00 CET. The third public ticket sales phase will open on March 17 at 10:00 CET, while the final sales phase will begin on April 20 at 10:00 CEST, coinciding with the conclusion of the Regular Season as teams continue their pursuit of a Final Four berth.

Each remaining phase will feature a limited allocation across multiple price categories, and high demand is expected once again.

All ticketing details for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens Presented by Etihad are available at [https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/](https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/).

articleMetadata.title: Phase 2 general public tickets for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad sell out
articleMetadata.description: The extraordinary demand for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens Presented by Etihad shows no signs of slowing down, as all tickets made available during Phase 2 of public sales have officially sold out.

heroMedia.title: Jared Butler’s blitz and inside dominance fuels Zvezda's victory over Efes
heroMedia.summary: The Serbian team kept on pushing for a top-six finish with a hard-earned win

linkedIds.text: Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade v Anadolu Efes Istanbul (2026-02-25T19:30:00.000Z)

content.text:
[Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/crvena-zvezda-meridianbet-belgrade/red/) showed two distinct offensive faces to hold off [Anadolu Efes Istanbul](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/anadolu-efes-istanbul/ist/) and grab a key 91-81 home victory on Wednesday night.

The first aspect of the Serbian team’s play was shooting from beyond the arc, led by [Jared Butler](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/jared-butler/014163/) firing 7-of-9 from three-point range. He was supported by [Nikola Kalinic](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/nikola-kalinic/004866/) with 2 of his 3 attempts, while [Codi Miller-McIntyre](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/codi-miller-mcintyre/009048/) also made a pair and [Ognjen Dobric](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/ognjen-dobric/004007/) stringed his only long-range shot, allowing the team to make a more than creditable 12-of-24 three-pointers (50%).

However, that Butler-inspired barrage from deep was never enough to establish a game-clinching lead against an Efes team that played with balance and discipline to stay in contention nearly until the final buzzer. Although Zvezda led all the way after moving ahead, 35-32, with a Kalinic triple 3 minutes before halftime, the margin only reached a level of comfort late in the fourth quarter – and that was nothing to do with long-range shooting.

content.title: Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade-Anadolu Efes Istanbul | Round 29 Highlights | 2025-26 EuroLeague

content.text:
Instead, Sasa Obradovic’s team gradually pulled into a dominant lead through a final period of inside dominance at both ends of the floor. Between Kalinic making his second triple with 2:49 to play in the third quarter, and Butler draining his seventh with just over a minute remaining, the home team did not make a single three-point strike.

During that phase of nearly 12 minutes, Zvezda turned its attention to aggressive drives to the basket, scoring 7 two-pointers with [Chima Moneke](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/chima-moneke/012201/) and [Jordan Nwora](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/jordan-nwora/013363/) both netting three-point plays. At the same time, Zvezda also flexed its defensive muscles, holding Efes to only one field goal conversion during the middle 6 minutes of the fourth quarter.

Although Efes managed to compete physically for much of the game, Zvezda’s gradually increasing physical dominance allowed the home team to come away with a 41-32 rebounding edge, including 13 offensive rebounds, which resulted in 12 second-chance points.

МВП, статистика и најистакнутији појединци утакмице 29. кола @EuroLeague 📊🔴⚪️#kkcz #WeAreTheTeam pic.twitter.com/bsNtlxJGEy

— KK Crvena zvezda Meridianbet (@kkcrvenazvezda) February 25, 2026

The victory was orchestrated by Miller-McIntyre, whose overall contribution was apparent from his combination of 12 points, 6 rebounds and 6 assists with just 1 turnover. And the point guard was clear about the key to his team’s triumph, pinpointing Zvezda’s success in turning back an Efes third-quarter burst of 25 points in just over 8 minutes.

“Efes is a good team with great players and they’re going to make a run, which they did,” explained Miller-McIntyre. “But we were able to stay calm in those moments and come out with the win. If we can get stops and run, we’re phenomenal. But we need to know when to slow it down, when to push the pace, and I think we did a great job with that tonight.

“When we focus on defense, I think we can beat anybody, or at least be in the game with anybody, and when they made that run, we found a way to get stops and convert on the offensive side.”

It was an important win for Zvezda, which snapped a two-game losing streak in the EuroLeague and came immediately after the emotional high of a Serbian Cup triumph last weekend.

❤️🤍 https://t.co/EFRmjqdg52

— KK Crvena zvezda Meridianbet (@kkcrvenazvezda) February 25, 2026

Coach Obradovic admitted his players were fatigued after the efforts they exerted to take that trophy, but he hailed their ability to close out the game in the crucial stages, telling EuroLeague TV: “We missed physicality, but in some important moments we found a way, especially with the defense. The effort was over the line in this moment.

“In the last quarter, we did great things, and again it shows the character of the team.”

Efes coach Pablo Laso, meanwhile, insisted his team will not give up on the season despite being seven wins away from the play-in places.

“We are representing a club that always fights, and needs to fight, until the end in every game,” he said in the postgame press conference.

“We have to continue playing the best way possible. The play-in looks very far away, for sure, but I don’t understand another way to be a better basketball team than this. So we have to keep fighting, game by game.”

articleMetadata.title: Butler’s blitz and inside dominance fuels Zvezda's victory over Efes
articleMetadata.description: Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade showed two distinct offensive faces to hold off Anadolu Efes Istanbul and grab a key 91-81 home victory on Wednesday night, led by Jared Butler firing 7-of-9 from three-point range.

heroMedia.title: Power rankings: After the cups have gone
heroMedia.summary: Our experts offer up a suggestion for every team through 28 rounds

content.text:
Welcome to the latest edition of the EuroLeague Power Rankings. The list is the brainchild of [EuroLeague.net](http://EuroLeague.net)'s team of experts, which is made up of Geoff Gillingham, Javier Gancedo, Frankie Sachs and Igor Petrinovic. The rankings are not a prediction of what the final standings will look like, but rather where all 20 teams stand – in their humble opinions – today, after Round 28.

 

### 1. Fenerbahce Beko Istanbul (20-7)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Besiktas on Sunday to win the Turkish Cup

**The question we are asking:** Is Fenerbahce aware that not team to finish the regular season in first place under the current format has won the championship?

 

### 2. Olympiacos Piraeus (18-9)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Panathinaikos in the Greek Cup final on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Do we need to start worrying about the health of the Reds’ stars?

 

### 3. Real Madrid (17-11)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Baskonia in the Spanish Cup final on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** Can *Los Blancos* improve its defense to become a top contender?

### 4. Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Olympiacos to win the Greek Cup on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Will the addition of Nigel Hayes-Davis be enough to put the Greens over the top?

 

### 5. Valencia Basket (18-10)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Real Madrid in the Spanish Cup semifinal on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** How will Valencia shake its disappointment from not winning the Spanish Cup at home? 

 

### 6. FC Barcelona (17-11)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Barcelona in the Spanish Cup semifinal on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** With Punter back in action, can Barça improve its consistency and stay in the top-six race?

 

### 7. Zalgiris Kaunas (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Rytas Vilnius in the Lithuanian Cup final on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** With six home games remaining and good momentum, is a top-six finish a realistic goal?

### 8. AS Monaco (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Le Mans to win the French Leaders Cup on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** After a turbulent stretch on and off the court, can this trophy galvanize Monaco and stabilize its EuroLeague push?

 

### 9. Hapoel IBI Tel Aviv (16-11)

**What they did last week:** Went 3-0 in the Israeli League

**The question we are asking:** Is Hapoel’s recent slide just a bump in the road or a sign that the team peaked too early?

 

### 10. Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Mega Belgrade to win the Serbian Cup on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Is Jordan Nwora the star who can carry Zvezda to new heights? 

 

### 11. Dubai Basketball (14-14)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Bosna Sarajevo in the ABA League

**The question we are asking:** Now that Dzanan Musa is back and in shape, can Dubai win enough games to make it to the Play-In Showdown?

### 12. Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv (13-15)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Bnei Herzliya to win the Israeli Cup on Thursday

**The question we are asking:** Is there enough time left in the season for Maccabi to climb to 10th place?

 

### 13. EA7 Emporio Armani Milan (14-14)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Tortona to win the Italian Cup on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** Can Milan translate domestic momentum into EuroLeague consistency and revive its play-in hopes?

 

### 14. FC Bayern Munich (12-16)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Bamberg in German Cup semifinals on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** What would this team have looked like with a full season under Coach Pesic?

 

### 15. Anadolu Efes Istanbul (9-19)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Besiktas in the Turkish Cup semifinal on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Is Efes destined to become a spoiler for a few teams in the playoff race?

### 16. Virtus Bologna (12-16)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Tortona in the Italian Cup semifinals on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** What adjustments can Dusko Ivanovic make to halt Virtus’s slide – especially away from home?

 

### 17. Partizan Mozzart Bet Belgrade (9-19)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Mega Belgrade in the Serbian Cup semifinals on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Can Joan Penarroya realign the locker room and restore Partizan’s competitive edge?

 

### 18. Paris Basketball (9-18)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Bourg in the French Leader’s Cup quarterfinals on Friday

**The question we are asking:** What will it take for Paris to recapture its early-season identity and get Nadir Hifi back to his best?

 

### 19. Kosner Baskonia Vitoria-Gasteiz (9-19)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Real Madrid in the Spanish Cup final on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** After its Spanish Cup success and strong home performances, can Baskonia start winning consistently on the road?

 

### 20. LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne (7-21)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Monaco in the French Cup quarterfinals on Tuesday

**The question we are asking:** With the postseason out of reach, which players are auditioning for their EuroLeague futures?

heroMedia.title: 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad – Ticket sales Phase 2 to start on February 26
heroMedia.summary: Phase 1 general public ticket sale attracted over 1 million fans worldwide

content.text:
Ticket sales for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad will enter their next chapter on February 26 at 10:00 CET, as Phase 2 opens to the general public.

The launch of Phase 2 coincides with the closing of the EuroLeague players’ market, ensuring that fans will have a clear picture of each team’s final roster.

All fans,  except those who have already purchased tickets during previous sales phases or pre-sales , will be eligible to access the February 26 general public sale starting at 10:00 CET. During this second sales window,  all fans will also have the opportunity to purchase VIP tickets starting at 14:00 CET .

Ticketing information is available at [https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/](https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/). Fans must log in or create a EuroLeague ID account to complete their purchase. Once logged in, all available categories will be displayed. Due to expected high demand and to ensure fairness, each fan may purchase a maximum of two tickets.

For the first time, Final Four tickets are being released in multiple sales phases. This approach is designed to expand access for the EuroLeague’s growing global fan base, offering multiple opportunities to secure tickets,  whether fans plan to attend regardless of the qualified teams or prefer to wait until the competition reaches its decisive stages. All phases follow a fair access policy, meaning that fans who purchase tickets in one phase will not be eligible to participate in subsequent phases. This measure is designed to ensure broader access to the event.

Phase 1, held on February 11, experienced massive demand, as over a million fans connected to sold out all available tickets in less than five minutes, once again demonstrating the enormous appeal of the EuroLeague’s premier event.

The 2026 EuroLeague Final Four semifinals will be played on Friday, May 22, at 18:00 and 21:00 local time (17:00 and 20:00 CEST). On Sunday, May 24, the adidas NextGen EuroLeague Championship Game will tip off at 18:00 local time (17:00 CEST), while the season will culminate with the Championship Game at 21:00 local time (20:00 CEST), when a new EuroLeague champion will be crowned.

A third ticket sales window will open on Tuesday, March 17, at 10:00 CET, offering another opportunity for fans to secure their seats. The final sales phase will launch on Monday, April 20, at 10:00 CEST, positioned between the conclusion of the Regular Season and the start of the Play-In Showdown, with 10 teams still in contention for a place in the Final Four.

All ticketing details for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad are available at [https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/](https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/).

heroMedia.title: Serbian Cup: Crvena Zvezda dominates to sixth straight crown

content.text:
Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade defeated Mega Belgrade 106-84 to win the Serbian Cup for the sixth consecutive time. It is Zvezda’s 10th Serbian Cup win in the last 14 years, and 12th in competition history.

Zvezda scored 65 first-half points, then never lost control of the game in the second half. Jordan Nwora scored 23 of his 29 points in the first half, and Ognjen Dobric netted 21 points, making 7 triples on 9 attempts, to lead Zvezda which finished the game making 17 three-pointers on 48.6% shooting.

Jared Butler had 11, while Ebuka Izundu, Uros Plavsic and Stefan Miljenovic each scored 10 points in a win, with Izundu also pulling down 11 rebounds. Luigi Suigo collected 23 points and 8 rebounds in defeat.

Mega scored the first 7 points of the game, but Zvezda put together an 18-2 run late in the first quarter to open a double-digit lead, then a 21-8 second quarter spurt to pull away to a 60-43 lead. Mega did get within 79-70 late in the third quarter, but Dobric ended all Mega’s hopes of a miracle comeback, as Zvezda celebrated winning its first trophy of the season.

articleMetadata.title: Serbian Cup: Crvena Zvezda dominates to sixth straight crown

heroMedia.title: Serbian Cup: Zvezda reaches the final
heroMedia.summary: Zvezda beat Spartak to stay alive in the competition. It will face Mega in the final.

content.text:
### Nwora, Miller-McIntyre lead Zvezda to the final

Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade reached the Serbian Radivoj Korac Cup final by downing Spartak Office Shoes 86-69 in front of 4,500 fans at SC Cair Arena. As such, Zvezda remains in the fight to win a sixth consecutive Serbian Cup record, a feat unprecedented since the country gained independence. 

[Jordan Nwora](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/jordan-nwora/013363/) led the winners with 22 points. [Codi Miller-McIntyre](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/codi-miller-mcintyre/009048/) added 20 while [Ebuka Izundu](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/ebuka-izundu/014029/) had 11 for Zvezda, which limited Spartak to just 15 of 40 two-pointers (37.5% 2FG) in this game. 

Zvezda had a strong start, as Miller-McIntyre struck from downtown for an early 11-4 lead. Miller-McIntyre got help from Izundu to keep Zvezda way ahead, 20-13, after 10 minutes. Spartak managed to take the lead, 29-30, on a three-pointer by Ivan Drobnjak. [Jared Butler](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/jared-butler/014163/) and Miller-McIntyre helped Zvezda regain a 38-37 edge at halftime. Spartak managed to build a seven-point lead, 49-56, late in the third quarter. Nwora, Butler and [Nikola Kalinic](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/nikola-kalinic/004866/) fueled a game-changing 22-0 run that put Zvezda ahead for good, 71-56, as Spartak did not recover in the final 7 minutes.

Рампа Калине и тројка @JordanNwora за потврду победе! 👌🏼🔴⚪️#kkcz #WeAreTheTeam pic.twitter.com/SLP12eWj5E

— KK Crvena zvezda Meridianbet (@kkcrvenazvezda) February 20, 2026

Zvezda is set to face Mega Superbet Belgrade, which downed Partizan Mozzart Bet Belgrade 68-80 in the second semifinal. Bogoljub Markovic led Mega with 17 points. [Bruno Fernando](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/bruno-fernando/013667/) and [Duane Washington](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/duane-washington/013370/) led Partizan with 12 points apiece while [Isaac Bonga](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/isaac-bonga/006760/) added 11. Partizan led 36-35 near halftime, but Mega used a 2-14 run to take full control of the game, sealing the deal long before the final buzzer.

Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade reached the Serbian Radivoj Korac Cup final by downing Spartak Office Shoes 86-69 in front of 4,500 fans at SC Cair Arena. As such, Zvezda stays in the fight to win a sixth consecutive Serbian Cup record, something unprecedented since the country became independent. 

Jordan Nwora led the winners with 22 points. Codi Miller-McIntyre added 20 while Ebuka Izundu had 11 for Zvezda, which limited Spartak to just 15 of 40 two-pointers (37.5% 2FG) in this game.
